purpose: Verify signed webhook payloads using provider-specific HMAC rules before business logic processes incoming events.

endpoints.description: Verify a raw webhook body and signature header using a configured provider verification strategy.

sidecar_specification.purpose: Incoming webhook signature verification for common HMAC-based providers.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use at the very start of webhook handling, before parsing or acting on the event payload.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use for OAuth bearer token validation, mutual TLS, or post-verification event deduplication.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Capture the raw request body exactly as received. 2) Call /webhooks/verify-signature with provider, headers, and secret. 3) Parse and process only when valid is true.

failure_modes.condition: Raw body was modified before verification
failure_modes.symptom: valid false even though the sender secret is correct
failure_modes.recovery: Verify against the exact raw UTF-8 payload before JSON parsing or proxy rewriting

failure_modes.condition: Timestamp or signature is outside tolerance
failure_modes.symptom: valid false with replay or signature mismatch error
failure_modes.recovery: Use the correct secret and verify within the accepted replay window

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Use Node crypto createHmac and timingSafeEqual. Implement provider-specific header parsing for GitHub X-Hub-Signature-256 and Stripe-Signature style timestamped schemes. Treat raw_body as UTF-8 exactly as received.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Webhook frameworks often parse or reserialize JSON before verification, which breaks signatures. Constant-time comparison matters.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Keep provider verifiers small and explicit, returning structured verification errors instead of throwing.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not verify parsed JSON, do not strip whitespace, and do not accept missing signature headers.
